metadata.title: Spanish/English Language Assurance for AI, Product & CX Teams | Xpandia
metadata.description: Xpandia helps AI, SaaS, EdTech, Product, Localization, and CX teams validate Spanish and English quality across AI outputs, product experiences, support content, and launch-ready journeys.
metadata.ogTitle: Spanish/English Language Assurance for AI, Product & CX Teams | Xpandia
metadata.ogDescription: Xpandia helps AI, SaaS, EdTech, Product, Localization, and CX teams validate Spanish and English quality across AI outputs, product experiences, support content, and launch-ready journeys.

hero.subheadline: Xpandia helps AI, SaaS, EdTech, Product, Localization, and CX teams evaluate Spanish quality across AI outputs, product experiences, support content, and launch-ready journeys.
hero.body: We turn expert Spanish judgment into measurable findings your team can act on: scorecards, issue taxonomies, severity ratings, readiness recommendations, and remediation guidance.

proofPoints.title: Decision-ready evidence
proofPoints.desc: Scorecards, issues taxonomy findings readout, risk analysis

positioning.headline: Spanish quality is too important to leave unmeasured.
positioning.body1: English and Spanish-speaking users experience your product through language: the answers your AI gives, the instructions your product provides, the support content they trust, and the messages that guide their decisions.
positioning.body2: When language quality is inconsistent, unnatural, culturally misaligned, or hard to understand, teams lose clarity, trust, adoption, and confidence before they even know where the issue started.
positioning.body3: Language Assurance gives your team a structured way to evaluate what is working, what needs attention, how severe each issue is, and whether the experience is ready for real users.
positioning.callout: The goal is simple: help your team know whether your Spanish/English is accurate, natural, trustworthy, culturally appropriate, and ready to ship.

whatWeEvaluate.headline: Language quality across every user-facing surface.
whatWeEvaluate.intro: Language Assurance can be applied to any Spanish and English experience your users see, read, hear, follow, or rely on.

cards.title: AI-generated outputs
cards.desc: Chatbot responses, AI tutors, copilots, agents, support replies, generated explanations, summaries, recommendations, and other model outputs in Spanish.

cards.title: Product experiences
cards.desc: Onboarding flows, UI copy, forms, empty states, error messages, notifications, tooltips, checkout flows, and release-critical product surfaces.

cards.title: Support and CX content
cards.desc: Help center articles, support macros, chatbot flows, escalation language, knowledge base content, and customer-facing communication.

cards.title: Marketing and conversion content
cards.desc: Landing pages, campaign copy, email flows, pricing pages, value propositions, trust messaging, and Spanish conversion paths.

cards.title: Localization quality
cards.desc: Translated or localized experiences that need expert review, consistency checks, regional fit, terminology alignment, or final sign-off.

cards.title: Launch readiness
cards.desc: Spanish product surfaces, AI experiences, or customer journeys that need validation before launch, relaunch, deployment, or scale.

whenToUse.headline: Use Language Assurance when your Spanish or English quality needs a decision.
items: Your AI generates Spanish/English outputs, but your team needs to know if users can trust them.
Your product is translated or localized, but you are unsure if it feels natural, clear, and ready.
Your team is preparing a Spanish/English launch, relaunch, release, or market expansion.
Your support, help center, or chatbot experience in Spanish/English needs quality validation.
Your team relies on vendors, reviewers, or internal speakers without a clear QA framework.
Your leadership team needs evidence, not opinions, before deciding what to fix or ship.

qualityCriteria.headline: The signals behind Spanish your team can trust.
qualityCriteria.intro: Each engagement is scoped around your product, audience, use case, and risk level. Depending on the project, we evaluate the criteria that matter most for your Spanish experience.

cards.title: Accuracy
cards.desc: Does the Spanish preserve the intended meaning, facts, instructions, and product logic?

cards.title: Clarity
cards.desc: Can users understand what to do, what is happening, and what the product is asking from them?

cards.title: Naturality
cards.desc: Does the language sound fluent, native, and appropriate for real Spanish-speaking users?

cards.title: Tone and register
cards.desc: Does the voice match your brand, audience, context, and level of formality?

cards.title: Terminology
cards.desc: Are product terms, feature names, support language, and key concepts consistent and usable?

cards.title: Regional fit
cards.desc: Does the Spanish align with the intended audience: LatAm, US Hispanic, Spain, or a specific regional variant?

cards.title: Cultural fit
cards.desc: Does the experience respect cultural expectations, trust signals, context, and audience perception?

cards.title: Instruction-following
cards.desc: Does the Spanish output follow the user request, product instructions (KB), task constraints, system guardrails, and expected response format?

cards.title: UX friction
cards.desc: Does language create confusion, hesitation, errors, abandonment, or unnecessary cognitive load?

cards.title: AI behavior
cards.desc: For AI outputs, does the system follow instructions, stay consistent, avoid risky language, and respond appropriately in Spanish?

cards.title: Readiness
cards.desc: Is the experience ready to ship, needs targeted fixes, or requires deeper remediation before release?

coreSolutions.headline: Focused audits and advisory for teams that need confidence before shipping Spanish experiences.

cards.name: AI Quality Audit Sprint
cards.bestFor: Teams with AI outputs, chatbot responses, tutors, copilots, agents, or automated support flows operating in Spanish and English.
cards.outcome: Understand quality risks, scoring, issue patterns, and readiness before users rely on the experience.

cards.name: Spanish Launch Readiness Audit
cards.bestFor: Teams launching or preparing a Spanish or English website, app, onboarding flow, support journey, AI experience, or customer-facing product surface.
cards.outcome: Identify what affects clarity, trust, UX, cultural fit, conversion, and readiness before launch or relaunch.

cards.name: Language Quality Advisory
cards.bestFor: Teams that need ongoing senior Spanish/English quality judgment, review workflows, QA standards, vendor/reviewer alignment, terminology guidance, or recurring quality decisions.
cards.outcome: Build a practical Spanish/English quality system your team can use to make better decisions, reduce review friction, and scale quality with confidence.

methodology.headline: A structured path from <u>Spanish review</u> to <u>release confidence</u>.

steps.title: Scope the surface
steps.body: We define your audience, Spanish variant, product surface, AI use case, content type, business objective, risk level, and timeline.

steps.title: Evaluate quality
steps.body: We review the selected material using expert Spanish/English judgment and structured criteria: accuracy, clarity, naturalness, tone, terminology, regional fit, cultural fit, UX friction, and readiness.

steps.title: Score and classify
steps.body: We organize findings by severity, impact, pattern, and implementation effort so your team can understand what matters most.

steps.title: Deliver the evidence
steps.body: You receive decision-ready findings your team can use immediately: scorecards, annotated examples, issue logs, severity ratings, prioritized recommendations, rewrites, standards, and executive readouts.

steps.title: Decide the next move
steps.body: We recommend the right path forward: ship, fix, retest, adapt, train, govern, or scale.

aiEcosystem.headline: AI models and platforms we validate and optimize.
aiEcosystem.intro: We do more than just review text; we deeply understand the automation, content generation, and low-code development environments where you build your products. We ensure the outputs of your agents, prompts, and integrated workflows are seamless, natural, and conversion-ready.

deliverables.headline: Evidence your team can use across product, AI, localization, and CX.
deliverables.intro: Language Assurance turns subjective feedback into structured findings your team can prioritize, share, and execute.

cards.title: Quality Scorecard
cards.desc: A measurable view of Spanish quality across the criteria that matter for your use case.

cards.title: Issue Taxonomy
cards.desc: A structured map of issue types, patterns, and recurring quality risks.

cards.title: Severity Ratings
cards.desc: Clear prioritization so your team knows what blocks launch, what should be fixed soon, and what can be improved over time.

cards.title: Annotated Examples
cards.desc: Specific examples that show where quality breaks down and how it affects the user experience.

cards.title: Remediation Guidance
cards.desc: Practical recommendations, rewrites, terminology notes, and next steps to help your team improve quickly.

cards.title: Executive Readout
cards.desc: A concise summary for Product, AI, Localization, CX, Support, Marketing, or leadership teams.

cards.title: Readiness Recommendation
cards.desc: A clear assessment of whether the Spanish experience is ready to ship, needs targeted fixes, or requires deeper remediation.

scorecardPreview.headline: Evidence, not opinions.
scorecardPreview.body: Every engagement ends with a quality scorecard your Product, AI, Localization and CX leaders can read in minutes and act on this quarter.
scorecardPreview.scorecardFooter: READINESS: NEEDS TARGETED FIXES · USE CASE: SUPPORT COPILOT · VARIANT: es-MX · REVIEWER: XPANDIA SENIOR

cards.desc: Validate Spanish outputs from chatbots, copilots, tutors, agents, and generative AI experiences.

pricing.headline: Start with the level of assurance your team needs now.
pricing.intro: Language Assurance engagements are scoped based on your product surface, audience, Spanish variant, sample size, complexity, risk level, and timeline.
pricing.note: Final pricing is confirmed after a short diagnostic call and scope review.

cards.title: Diagnostic
cards.desc: Get a fast expert read on your Spanish surface before choosing the right audit, sprint, or quality roadmap.

cards.title: Launch Readiness Audit
cards.desc: Evaluate a Spanish product, website, onboarding flow, support journey, or customer-facing experience before launch or relaunch.

cards.title: AI QA Sprint
cards.desc: Validate AI-generated Spanish outputs before users rely on them.

cards.title: Language Sign-Off / LSO
cards.desc: Get a final expert review and release recommendation before publishing or deploying a Spanish experience.

cards.title: Fractional Advisory
cards.desc: Bring senior Spanish quality leadership into your team without a full-time hire.

items.q: Is Language Assurance the same as translation?
items.a: No. Language Assurance evaluates whether existing Spanish outputs, content, product flows, or localized experiences are accurate, natural, consistent, culturally appropriate, and ready for users.
Yes. We evaluate AI-generated Spanish from chatbots, copilots, tutors, support agents, assistants, and other generative AI experiences.

items.q: Do you only review LatAm Spanish?
items.a: No. We can scope the engagement around the Spanish variant your product needs: LatAm, US Hispanic, Spain, neutral Spanish, or a specific regional market.

items.q: What do we need to provide?
items.a: Usually a representative sample of outputs, product screens, flows, content, prompts, support responses, or localized assets, plus context about audience, product goals, and launch timeline.

items.q: Can you help us fix the issues too?
items.a: Yes. Depending on scope, we can provide rewrites, remediation guidance, terminology notes, reviewer guidance, or a follow-up localization/adaptation engagement.
Yes. Teams can start with an audit or sprint and move into fractional advisory, governance, recurring QA, vendor oversight, or quality roadmap support.

finalCta.headline: Know if your Spanish is ready before users decide for you.
finalCta.body: Book a diagnostic call to review your Spanish/English surface, audience, use case, timeline, and business goal. We'll recommend the right Language Assurance path: diagnostic, AI QA sprint, launch readiness audit, retest, sign-off, or advisory.

additionalModules.headline: Add the level of assurance your project needs.
additionalModules.body: Depending on scope, Language Assurance engagements can include conversational chatbot audits, AI output benchmarks, language sign-off, retesting, terminology checks, or lightweight governance support.
